title: lupinus argenteus var. moabensis
intro: Lupinus argenteus var. moabensis, also known as the Moab silvery lupine, is a variety of the silvery lupine plant, a perennial legume native to the southwestern United States. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a natural anti-inflammatory and antibacterial agent. The health benefits of Lupinus argenteus var. moabensis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including wounds, skin issues, and gastrointestinal problems.
